World Cup 2026 Predictions

An AI model's read on the World Cup 2026 — and how its predictions have actually held up. Below is GoalIQ's match-by-match track record so far, then model win probabilities and expected goals (xG) for the upcoming group games.

Model output as of 22 June 2026 · Group stage in progress · Updated as the tournament progresses.

Predict any match in the app →

Every pick below was the model's pre-kickoff 1X2 call (neutral-venue), shown against the actual result.

Knockout rounds: GoalIQ will publish neutral-venue predictions for the World Cup 2026 knockout bracket once the group stage finishes and the matchups are set.

How the model works

GoalIQ is powered by a Dixon-Coles statistical model combined with a machine-learning expected-goals (xG) ensemble, trained on thousands of real international and club matches. For any fixture it estimates the win / draw / win probability and the expected goals for each team. World Cup 2026 predictions are computed on a neutral-venue basis and the track record above updates as new results come in.

World Cup 2026 predictions: FAQ

How accurate has GoalIQ's World Cup 2026 model been so far?
Through 40 played World Cup 2026 matches, GoalIQ's model called the correct result in 21 of them (52%). About a third of group games (13 of 40) ended level — the model always names the more likely winner, and when a match produced a winner it picked the right side 21 times out of 27 (78%). Every pick shown was made before kick-off, on a neutral-venue basis. These are model estimates, not guarantees.
Which upcoming World Cup 2026 match is the biggest mismatch?
In the upcoming group games, GoalIQ's model's most one-sided call is Argentina against Jordan at a 84.1% win probability.
Which upcoming games are the closest?
The model sees Paraguay–Australia, Ecuador–Germany, Egypt–Iran, Cape Verde Islands–Saudi Arabia as the tightest of the upcoming games — no side above ~42% win probability, close to a coin-flip.
Will there be knockout-round predictions?
Yes. GoalIQ will publish neutral-venue predictions for the World Cup 2026 knockout rounds once the group stage finishes and the bracket is set.
How does GoalIQ predict football matches?
GoalIQ uses a Dixon-Coles statistical model combined with an expected-goals (xG) ensemble, trained on thousands of real international and club matches. It estimates win/draw/win probabilities and expected goals for any fixture. It is an informational analytics tool, not a betting service.
Is GoalIQ a betting app?
No. GoalIQ is an analytics and entertainment app that provides statistical model predictions for informational purposes only. It is not a betting or gambling service and does not provide betting tips or odds.
Get a prediction for every match →
Important: GoalIQ provides statistical model predictions for informational and entertainment purposes only. It is not a betting or gambling service and does not provide betting tips or odds. Predictions are estimates, not guarantees.
